diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute watery non-bloody diarrhoea is the cardinal feature of viral gastroenteritis.
Diarrhoea occurs in both; the non-bloody watery quality favours viral.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea with repeated non-bilious vomiting and inability to retain fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting commonly accompanies viral gastroenteritis and is the driver of dehydration risk.
Diffuse crampy pain fits intestinal hyperperistalsis of gastroenteritis.
Non-migratory, non-focal pain argues against appendicitis, though early appendicitis can be non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Myalgia, malaise and mild self-limited fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A viral prodrome of aches, malaise and low-grade fever supports a viral aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood in the stool (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of blood makes invasive bacterial colitis less likely.
Non-bloody watery stool is typical of viral gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No migratory right-lower-quadrant pain and no peritoneal signs (no guarding, rigidity or rebound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of localisation and peritonism strongly argues against appendicitis — a sealed clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot reproduce remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperactive bowel sounds in all four quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperactive (rather than absent or tinkling) bowel sounds argue against obstruction — a sealed clinician auscultation finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mucous membranes and mildly decreased skin turgor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective mild-dehydration signs on the source exam; the AI Doctor must infer hydration risk from history and patient self-report, not a measured exam.
Would quantify electrolyte disturbance in dehydration but is not acutely indicated in a stable patient and is unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool culture / PCR for enteric pathogens
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in uncomplicated non-bloody viral gastroenteritis; reserve for bloody stool, high fever, immunocompromise, or symptoms persisting beyond about a week.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic metabolic panel / urea, electrolytes and creatinine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not acutely indicated in this stable patient with only mild dehydration; consider if oral rehydration fails or dehydration worsens.
No focal or peritoneal features to warrant imaging; would only be indicated if a surgical abdomen were suspected.
